mission: Design integration standardization + security hardening for 6 integration packages

root_causes.hypothesis: No pre-deploy security review gate → enterprise shipped with permissive CORS, no auth, plaintext secrets
evidence: cors() with no options = any origin (enterprise/src/routes/api/[...path].ts:12)
Authentication via randomUUID only — cryptographically meaningless (enterprise/src/core/share.ts:117-128)
Storage credentials from process.env with non-null assertions — no fallback, no validation (enterprise/src/core/storage.ts:67-72)
Deployed to production via SST (stage: Teams) — infr/enterprise.ts confirms active deployment
x-forwarded-host trusted blindly (enterprise/src/routes/api/[...path].ts:55-56)

root_causes.hypothesis: No promotion gate for prototypes → Slack never graduated from spike to hardened integration
evidence: Single-file architecture (src/index.ts, 146 lines) — never decomposed
3 HIGH security vectors: secrets on startup log (line 11-14), raw Slack context as JSON (line 63), raw opencode response as JSON (line 117)
Zero consumers — no forcing function to harden
Zero tests — no test directory exists
Last meaningful commit was a re-org (e8f155c1e) — no active development
Sessions Map grows unbounded with no pruning (line 22)

root_causes.hypothesis: No integration template/scaffold extracts http-recorder patterns → each integration reinvents security from scratch
evidence: http-recorder has Effect Layer, composable Redactor, secretFindings() scanning, safeText in diffs — gold standard
Slack uses console.log with no redaction — opposite of http-recorder's pattern
Enterprise has no secret scanning, no composable security primitives
No shared @opencode-ai/integration-security or equivalent package extracting http-recorder's patterns
Timeline unknown: if http-recorder was built after Slack/Enterprise, retroactive backfill never happened

root_causes.hypothesis: No formal deprecation lifecycle → abandoned packages rot in place with partial cleanup
evidence: Identity: directory does not exist on disk (packages/identity/ → 404). Package is gone.
No @opencode-ai/identity references anywhere in codebase — already fully removed
No DEPRECATED.md, no CHANGELOG deprecation entry, no formal deprecation signal for any package
Slack: still in root package.json workspaces array despite zero consumers and zero development

root_causes.hypothesis: Cross-cutting: Integration lifecycle governance is missing — no scaffold, no promote gate, no deploy review, no deprecation process
evidence: Create phase: no integration scaffold with security defaults
Promote phase: no quality bar (test coverage, security scan) required to graduate
Deploy phase: no security review gate before production deployment
Retire phase: no formal deprecation process — Slack still listed as workspace package

fixes.title: Enterprise: CORS tightening + x-forwarded-host validation
fixes.description: Restrict CORS to known origins via environment configuration. Validate x-forwarded-host against allowed hosts list. This is the highest-urgency fix — deployed security vulnerability on live production endpoint.

tests_to_run: packages/enterprise/test/core/ (existing tests)
New: CORS rejection test for unauthorized origins
New: CORS allowance test for known origins
consumers_affected: None — zero runtime imports from production code

risks: MEDIUM: Tightening CORS could break existing integrations calling the API from unlisted domains. Must audit current callers before deploying.
LOW: If OPENCODE_CORS_ORIGINS env var is empty or malformed, all requests will be rejected. Need default fallback.
validation: 1. bun test: verify 403 for cross-origin request from unlisted origin
2. bun test: verify 200 for allowed origin
3. Deploy to staging, verify existing share integrations still work
4. Check infra/enterprise.ts for env var wiring

fixes.title: Enterprise: x-forwarded-host validation
fixes.description: Validate that x-forwarded-host header matches a known safe hostname list before using it in URL construction. Prevent host header injection.
fixes.before:
const protocol = c.req.header("x-forwarded-proto") ?? c.req.header("x-forwarded-protocol") ?? "https"
      const host = c.req.header("x-forwarded-host") ?? c.req.header("host")

tests_to_run: New: host injection rejection test (x-forwarded-host: evil.com)
New: allowed host acceptance test (x-forwarded-host: opencode.ai)

risks: LOW: If deployed behind a proxy that sends unexpected x-forwarded-host values, share URLs will break. Must configure OPENCODE_ALLOWED_HOSTS per environment.
validation: 1. bun test: verify 400 for x-forwarded-host: attacker.com
2. bun test: verify 200 for x-forwarded-host: opencode.ai
3. bun test: verify 200 for host header: opencode.ai (no x-forwarded)
4. Deploy to staging, verify share creation still returns correct URLs

fixes.title: Enterprise: HMAC-based share authentication (replace randomUUID)
fixes.description: Replace randomUUID-based secret with HMAC-SHA256 derived from a server-side signing key + shareID. This prevents secret guessing while maintaining stateless verification. The share secret becomes a verifiable token rather than a database-stored random string.

affected_files: packages/enterprise/src/routes/api/[...path].ts (no change — secret is passed through unchanged)
tests_to_run: New: verify share secret validation
New: verify HMAC verification rejects tampered secrets
New: verify backward compatibility with existing shares (if any)
consumers_affected: Any clients storing share secrets — secrets will be longer (HMAC hex ≈ 64 chars vs UUID ≈ 36 chars)

risks: HIGH: Breaking change — existing share URLs with old UUID secrets will stop working. Need migration window or dual-verify strategy.
MEDIUM: If OPENCODE_SHARE_SIGNING_KEY rotates, all existing shares become invalid. Need key rotation support.
MEDIUM: Share secret length increases (64 chars vs 36). Must verify clients can handle longer secrets.
MEDIUM: Current secret comparison (share.ts:139) uses non-constant-time !== operator — vulnerable to timing attack. Must use timingSafeEqual or equivalent for secret verification.
fixes.zoning_board_note: Finding 6: share.ts:139 uses standard string comparison for secrets — replace with constant-time comparison during HMAC migration.
validation: 1. bun test: create share → verify secret validates
2. bun test: create share → tamper secret → verify rejection
3. bun test: sync/delete with valid secret → verify success
4. bun test: sync/delete with invalid secret → verify 401

fixes.title: Enterprise: Remove plaintext secrets from storage adapter
fixes.description: Replace direct process.env access for AWS credentials with a lazy credential provider that supports Cloudflare Workers secrets bindings. On CF Workers, use ctx.env (secure binding). On local/other, fall back to process.env with a deprecation warning.

affected_files: infra/enterprise.ts (verify SST secret binding names match)
tests_to_run: New: verify storage adapter works with CF Workers env binding
New: verify storage adapter fails gracefully with missing credentials
New: verify credential provider logs deprecation warning for process.env fallback
Existing: any storage integration tests
consumers_affected: infra/enterprise.ts: SST config already uses SECRET.R2AccessKey and SECRET.R2SecretKey — no change needed there

risks: LOW: infra/enterprise.ts already uses SST secrets. The change is internal to storage.ts.
LOW: If CF Workers binding name differs from env var name, need to map. Check actual binding names.
validation: 1. bun test: verify s3() reads from CF Workers env binding when available
2. bun test: verify s3() falls back to process.env on non-Workers
3. bun test: verify explicit error when no credentials available (not just undefined crash)
4. Verify infra/enterprise.ts binding names match the new credential provider

fixes.title: Slack: Add log redaction to eliminate 3 HIGH data exposure vectors
fixes.description: Replace raw console.log calls with a structured logger that redacts secrets, message content, and API responses. Add a redactSlackContext() helper that strips sensitive fields before logging.
fixes.before: console.log("📡 Raw Slack event:", JSON.stringify(context, null, 2))

tests_to_run: New: verify SLACK_BOT_TOKEN does not appear in log output
New: verify user message content does not appear in log output
New: verify opencode AI response does not appear in log output
New: verify non-sensitive fields (channel ID, timestamp) DO appear

risks: LOW: Debug logging becomes less verbose — developer may miss context when debugging. Consider making redaction configurable (debug mode vs production).
LOW: If redactSlackContext() has a bug, it could crash the message handler. Must handle gracefully.
validation: 1. bun test: mock Slack event with token in context → verify redacted output
2. bun test: mock Slack message with user text → verify redacted output
3. bun test: verify channel ID and timestamp survive redaction
4. bun test: verify redaction handles null/undefined gracefully

fixes.title: Slack: Add session pruning with TTL to prevent unbounded memory growth
fixes.description: Add a periodic cleanup interval (every 5 minutes) that removes sessions older than a configurable TTL (default 1 hour). Use Slack message timestamp to determine session age.
fixes.before: const sessions = new Map<string, { channel: string; threadTs: string }>()

tests_to_run: New: verify sessions older than TTL are pruned
New: verify sessions within TTL are NOT pruned
New: verify pruneSessions handles empty Map

risks: LOW: If SESSION_TTL_MS is set too low, active conversations could lose their session mapping. Must ensure pruning only targets idle sessions.
LOW: createdAt timestamp must be set at session creation — not retroactively.
validation: 1. bun test: create session at T-2 hours, run prune → verify deletion
2. bun test: create session at T-5 minutes, run prune → verify survival
3. bun test: verify prune handles Map with zero entries

fixes.title: Slack: Add Slack request signature verification
fixes.description: Enable Slack's built-in request verification. The @slack/bolt package supports this natively — verify that signingSecret is configured and the Bolt app is verifying inbound events.

tests_to_run: New: verify Bolt app validates x-slack-signature header
New: verify app fails to start without SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET
New: verify requests with invalid signatures are rejected

risks: MEDIUM: @slack/bolt in Socket Mode may handle verification differently. Socket Mode connections use appToken for auth. The signingSecret is for HTTP endpoints only. Need to verify the actual verification path.
LOW: If the existing code already implicitly verifies (Bolt does it by default), this is a no-op confirmation. Verify first.
validation: 1. Review @slack/bolt Socket Mode documentation to confirm verification behavior
2. bun test: verify app throws/refuses when signing secret is missing
3. bun test: verify socket mode connection succeeds with valid credentials

fixes.title: Slack: Add test suite
fixes.description: Create a test suite covering: log redaction, session lifecycle, message handling, error paths. Use Bun test runner (matching repo convention). Mock Slack Bolt app to avoid real network calls.
fixes.after:
packages/slack/test/
├── index.test.ts        # Message handling, session mapping
├── redaction.test.ts    # Log redaction for all 3 HIGH vectors
└── session-lifecycle.test.ts  # Session creation, lookup, pruning

tests_to_run: bun test (from packages/slack)

risks: LOW: If Slack has zero consumers and is headed for deprecation, a test suite may be wasted effort. Consider fix-J (formal deprecation) before investing here.
validation: 1. bun test (packages/slack) — all tests pass
2. Verify test coverage > 80% for src/index.ts
3. Verify no network calls in test suite (pure mocks)

fixes.title: Standardization: Extract http-recorder security primitives into shared @opencode-ai/integration-security package
fixes.description: Extract secretFindings(), redactHeaders(), redactUrl(), safeText(), and the Redactor composable pattern into a new shared package. This package has zero runtime dependencies on http-recorder or any LLM infrastructure — it's pure security primitives. Enterprise and future integrations import this to gain proactive secret scanning and redaction.
fixes.before: // Each integration writes its own secret scanning, or has none at all

affected_files: packages/http-recorder/src/matching.ts (~5 line change — import from new package)
tests_to_run: packages/integration-security/test/ (new)
packages/http-recorder/test/record-replay.test.ts (verify no regression)
packages/llm/test/recorded-test.ts (verify http-recorder consumer)
packages/opencode/test/session/llm-native-recorded.test.ts (verify http-recorder consumer)
consumers_affected: llm test suite: uses http-recorder → verify no regression

risks: MEDIUM: Extracting shared code from http-recorder could introduce versioning issues. http-recorder's consumers (llm, opencode tests) must see no behavioral change.
MEDIUM: integration-security adds a new workspace package — CI must build, typecheck, and test it.
LOW: If http-recorder's redactHeaders depends on http-recorder-specific context, extraction may need adaptation.
validation: 1. bun typecheck (packages/integration-security) — no errors
2. bun test (packages/integration-security) — all tests pass
3. bun typecheck (packages/http-recorder) — no regression
4. bun test (packages/http-recorder) — all tests pass
5. bun test (packages/llm) — recorded tests still pass
6. bun test (packages/opencode) — llm-native-recorded.test.ts still passes

fixes.title: Slack: Formal deprecation (preferred over hardening if zero consumers hold)
fixes.description: Formally deprecate the Slack package. Add DEPRECATED.md with rationale and migration guidance. Remove from root package.json workspaces. Remove from codebase-graph.ts WORKSPACE_PACKAGES array. This is a ONE-WAY decision — either fix-E through fix-H (harden) OR fix-J (deprecate), not both.
files: packages/app/src/utils/codebase-graph.ts (remove slack entry from WORKSPACE_PACKAGES and PACKAGE_COLORS)
fixes.before: // Slack package exists but has zero consumers, zero tests, 3 HIGH security vectors

tests_to_run: bun typecheck (root) — verify no broken imports
bun typecheck (packages/app) — verify codebase-graph still builds
bun build (packages/app) — verify no runtime import failure
consumers_affected: None — zero runtime imports from any other package (confirmed by grep)

risks: LOW: If any dev has a local .env with SLACK_BOT_TOKEN and runs `bun dev` in packages/slack, it continues to work — deprecation doesn't break it, just signals no further maintenance.
LOW: Removing from codebase-graph.ts changes the visualization. Verify no dashboard/report depends on slack node existing.
validation: 1. bun typecheck (root) — zero errors
2. grep -r '@opencode-ai/slack' packages/ — only self-reference in packages/slack/package.json
3. bun dev (packages/slack) — still works if someone has credentials
4. Verify codebase graph visualization renders without slack node

fixes.title: Identity: Formal deprecation recording (phantom package — already removed)
fixes.description: The identity package directory does not exist on disk and has zero references anywhere in the codebase. It has already been physically removed but never formally recorded. Create a deletion record at docs/json/opencode/archived/identity-removal.v1.json documenting the removal.
fixes.before: (identity package is already gone — phantom reference in surface map only)
fixes.after: (formal record of removal exists for audit trail)
risks: LOW: If identity directory was moved/renamed rather than deleted, this could create confusion. Verified: packages/identity/ does not exist on disk.
validation: 1. ls packages/identity → no such file or directory (already true)
2. grep -r '@opencode-ai/identity' . → zero results (already true)
3. Verify removal record passes schema validation

fixes.title: Integration test framework: Create shared test utilities at packages/integration-security/test-utils
fixes.description: Create shared test infrastructure that provides: mock HTTP server (building on http-recorder patterns), standard test fixtures for integration packages, Effect Layer test helpers. This reduces the barrier to adding tests for integration packages.
fixes.before: // Each integration writes its own test infrastructure from scratch
fixes.after: // packages/integration-security/test-utils provides shared mocks, fixtures, and Effect Layer helpers

tests_to_run: bun test (packages/integration-security)
bun test (packages/enterprise) — verify adoption

risks: LOW: Test utilities add dependency on integration-security for integration packages that don't yet exist. This is forward-looking infrastructure.
validation: 1. bun test (packages/integration-security) — all tests pass
2. Verify README documents how to use test utilities
3. Verify enterprise can adopt without breaking existing tests

fixes.title: CI/CD: Add integration security lint gate
fixes.description: Add a CI step that validates integration packages against security requirements: no permissive CORS, auth middleware present, no plaintext secrets in env access patterns, secret scanning configured. This prevents future integrations from shipping with prototype-quality security.
fixes.before: (no security lint step exists in CI)
fixes.after:
# CI runs: bun run scripts/lint-integration-security.ts
# Checks: CORS origins, auth presence, secret patterns in integration packages

tests_to_run: Run lint script manually to verify it catches known violations
Verify CI passes after all fixes are applied
consumers_affected: All integration packages — lint runs on every PR

risks: MEDIUM: Lint script must be carefully scoped to integration packages only — not core infrastructure.
LOW: False positives could block legitimate PRs. Need clear override mechanism.
validation: 1. Run lint against current enterprise code → expect failures for CORS, auth
2. Apply fixes A-D, run lint again → expect pass
3. Run lint against http-recorder → expect pass (gold standard)
4. Verify lint is informational (warning) not blocking during transition period

apply_order: fix-K (identity: 5 min, no risk, pure record-keeping)
fix-A (enterprise CORS: 30 min, immediate security impact)
fix-B (enterprise host validation: 15 min, paired with fix-A)
fix-D (enterprise secrets: 30 min, removes plaintext credentials)
fix-C (enterprise auth: 60 min, highest-risk change — needs migration plan)
fix-I (integration-security package: 90 min, infrastructure for everything else)
fix-H (slack tests: 45 min, higher effort — consider fix-J instead)
fix-J (slack deprecation: 15 min, ALTERNATIVE to fix-E through fix-H)

decision_points.question: Slack: harden (fix-E through fix-H) OR deprecate (fix-J)?

options.rationale: If there are plans to revive Slack integration (user demand, roadmap item), apply fixes E-H.
options.risk: LOW — zero consumers, no production impact

options.rationale: Slack has zero consumers, zero development for 2 years, 3 HIGH security vectors, and no demand signal. Deprecation is the lowest-effort, highest-safety option. Apply fix-J only.
options.risk: LOW — formally acknowledges what is already true

decision_points.recommendation: Deprecate (fix-J). The package is a prototype that never graduated. The codebase already functions without it. Formal deprecation is honest and reduces attack surface.

decision_points.question: Enterprise auth migration: dual-verify or forced rotation?

options.rationale: Accept both old UUID secrets and new HMAC secrets for 30 days. New shares get HMAC secrets. After 30 days, reject UUID secrets. This gives existing shares time to be recreated.
options.effort: Add fallback check: if HMAC fails, try UUID comparison

options.rationale: Break all existing shares immediately. Only acceptable if there are ZERO production shares currently active.

decision_points.recommendation: Dual-verify with 30-day deprecation window. Audit current share count first.

plan.rollback_plan: Each fix is independently reversible. Apply in order. If fix-C (auth) causes issues, revert it and keep fixes A, B, D. Fix-I (integration-security) is safe to revert — http-recorder re-exports maintain backward compatibility. Fix-J (slack deprecation) is trivially reversible — add back to workspaces array.
